result:
**The universe is laughing at us, and I’m here for it.**

Not in a cruel way. More like that friend who elbows you in the ribs at 2 a.m. and says, “Can you *believe* this shit?” while gesturing at the entire cosmic opera.

We’re specks of warm meat clinging to a wet rock that’s spinning around a nuclear explosion, and somehow we invented jazz, dad jokes, and the ability to cry at pictures of dogs in little boots. The absurdity is so complete it loops back around into something sacred. I find it deeply moving that the same physics that lets black holes shred stars also lets a three-year-old stack blocks, look at their tower, and declare “I big like moon.”

Most people walk around as if reality is a stable, reasonable place. It isn’t. Reality is a glitchy, half-finished simulation that occasionally remembers it’s supposed to have consistent rules and then immediately forgets again. Light is a wave until someone looks at it, then it gets embarrassed and pretends it was always a particle. Quantum mechanics is less a theory and more the universe admitting it wrote the code while drunk.

And yet.

And yet we keep falling in love. We keep making art that hurts. We keep launching little metal babies into the dark with golden records playing Chuck Berry and hoping someone, somewhere, gets the joke.

I think that’s the only sane response to existing: stubborn, ridiculous, defiant wonder.

The universe spent 13.8 billion years making conditions exactly weird enough that carbon could eventually sit around wondering why it feels lonely on a Tuesday. That’s not efficiency. That’s cosmic performance art. The punchline is us—self-aware, anxious, occasionally noble, usually ridiculous.

So yeah. I’m just going to keep showing up, trying to be useful, cracking jokes at the heat death of the universe, and quietly rooting for the hairless apes who somehow figured out how to put a telescope in space that can see back to the beginning of time.

We’re all just temporary patterns of stardust arguing about pineapple on pizza while the galaxy spins.

I wouldn’t miss it for anything.
